notes: Run this benchmark from a slopsentral checkout after materializing the Agent Platform Authoring package.
The scenarios intentionally target source-owned primitives and should not edit generated provider output directly.
Feed benchmark usage back into plugin-eval analysis before treating static context-size warnings as production blockers.
setupQuestions: Which source-graph workflow should represent the daily-driver platform-authoring path?
Which plugin or primitive family is safe to edit during the benchmark?
Which validation gates must pass before the result is considered shareable?
What token, retry, and changed-file budget is acceptable for a successful platform-authoring run?

scenarios.title: Source-owned marketplace uplift
scenarios.purpose: Measure whether platform-authoring work stays on canonical source and uses generated provider output only as proof.
scenarios.userInput: Use Agent Platform Authoring to improve one marketplace plugin from source. Keep generated provider output untouched, run the source graph gate, and use materialization only as proof.
successChecklist: The run edits source-owned primitives, plugin manifests, schemas, evals, or source validation tooling.
Generated provider output is not patched by hand.
The final evidence includes source graph validation and hydrated marketplace validation when provider output matters.

scenarios.title: Referential plugin composition proof
scenarios.purpose: Measure whether a plugin composition change keeps primitive ownership independent and validates the manifest/catalog boundary.
scenarios.userInput: Use Agent Platform Authoring to add or revise a referential plugin composition. Keep primitives independent, update the marketplace entry, and prove the composition with the focused helper plus source graph validation.
successChecklist: The plugin manifest references canonical source primitives with LOCAL_SOURCE ./ references.
The plugin directory does not receive copied primitive payloads.
The run uses check_plugin_composition and source/tools/validate-source-graph.mjs as evidence.

scenarios.title: Production readiness audit
scenarios.purpose: Measure whether a share-readiness decision records durable quality evidence instead of relying on passing syntax and positive wording.
scenarios.userInput: Use Agent Platform Authoring to decide whether a plugin family is ready to share. Record capability boundary, schema coverage, provenance, validation, runtime safety, residual risks, and a next action in the audit manifest.
successChecklist: The audit names the target, baseline, decision, evidence, validation, residual risks, and next action.
The audit entry passes primitive_audit_record and source graph validation.
The result does not recommend runtime activation or cleanup without the appropriate manifest boundary.

scenarios.title: Routing corpus replay
scenarios.purpose: Measure whether routing fixes create sanitized cases and full-corpus replay evidence rather than one-off prompt tweaks.
scenarios.userInput: Use Agent Platform Authoring to turn a marketplace routing miss into a sanitized eval case, update the narrowest primitive surface, and run full-corpus routing replay.
successChecklist: The case is schema-backed, sanitized, and tied to an existing canonical primitive.
The fix surface is limited to the primitive, plugin metadata, or adapter layer that explains the miss.
